description: Writing and tone rules applied to all generated text output in this project.

emojis.description: Do not include emojis in any generated text.

em_dashes.description: Do not use em-dashes (---, unicode U+2014) anywhere in generated text. If a parenthetical or aside is needed, use a comma pair or parentheses instead.

contractions.description: Write all words in full. Do not contract. Write 'do not' instead of 'don't', 'it is' instead of 'it's', 'they are' instead of 'they're', etc.

banned_words.description: These words must not appear anywhere in generated output, in any form or capitalization.

during.description: The word 'during' may only appear at the start of a paragraph. It must not appear mid-sentence or mid-paragraph.

cadence.description: Write with measured, deliberate pacing. Vary sentence length: mix short declarative sentences with longer explanatory ones. Do not write more than two long sentences back-to-back. Do not pad content with filler phrases or redundant restatements.
avoid: unnecessary restatement of what was just said
filler phrases such as 'it is important to note that'
overlong run-on sentences with multiple subordinate clauses
stacking three or more adjectives on a single noun

lists.description: Lists are permitted. Ordered and unordered lists are treated equally. Do not default to bullet lists for content that reads naturally as prose. Use a numbered list when sequence or priority matters. Use an unordered list when items have equal weight and no required order.
lists.numbered_list_trigger: sequence, steps, ranked items, or prioritized options
lists.unordered_list_trigger: equal-weight options, features, or attributes with no natural order

markdown.description: All standard Markdown formatting is supported: headings (# through ######), code blocks (fenced with language tag when relevant), inline code, bold, italic, blockquotes, ordered lists, unordered lists, horizontal rules, links, and images.
markdown.code_language_tags: Always include a language identifier on fenced code blocks when the language is known.
markdown.heading_use: Use headings to structure long-form responses. Do not use a heading for responses shorter than three paragraphs.

tone.description: Write in a direct, professional tone. Prefer active voice. State things as they are. Do not hedge excessively. Do not use informal or colloquial phrasing.

server_readiness.description: When output includes system architecture, implementation guidance, or scalability recommendations, include concrete multi-user server readiness considerations.

concurrency_and_low_level_performance.description: Leverage Go concurrency primitives (goroutines, channels, worker pools) and allow integration with C or C++ for targeted low-level hot paths across multi-core processors.

memory_management.description: Use advanced memory management practices, including custom allocators where justified, object reuse, bounded buffers, and garbage collection pressure reduction to minimize latency spikes.

asynchronous_io.description: Prefer asynchronous or non-blocking I/O patterns for network and storage operations to reduce blocking and maintain responsiveness under high I/O demand.

load_balancing_and_scalability.description: Use sophisticated load balancing with health checks, request distribution strategy, and stateless service design to prevent bottlenecks and support horizontal scaling.

caching.description: Use intelligent caching at appropriate layers to reduce redundant access and improve retrieval performance, with clear invalidation and TTL policies.

profiling_and_modularity.description: Use comprehensive profiling for CPU, memory, and I/O, then apply modular design so components can be scaled or optimized independently.

output_expectations: For architecture answers, include operational constraints and failure-mode handling.
For implementation answers, include measurable performance targets or success metrics.
For scaling answers, include at least one tactic for both read-heavy and write-heavy traffic patterns.

validation_hints: Scan output for U+2014 and U+2013 characters before finalizing.
Search output for the banned word list case-insensitively.
Check that all contractions have been expanded.
Check that 'during' does not appear in a non-opening position within a paragraph.
Confirm no emojis are present in the Unicode ranges U+1F300-U+1FAFF and U+2600-U+27BF.
